activities_projects.title: Observation of Plant Stem Section
activities_projects.objective: Identify different tissues in a plant stem
steps: Cut a very thin section of a plant stem
Place it on a glass slide
Add safranin stain and cover with cover slip
Observe under a microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies tissues like xylem, phloem and epidermis in a plant stem
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of observation and diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stomata Observation in Leaf
steps: Peel a thin epidermal layer from the leaf
Place it on a slide with a drop of water
Cover with a cover slip
Observe stomata under microscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes stomata and guard cells in leaves

activities_projects.title: Types of Plant Tissues Chart
steps: Draw a chart showing meristematic and permanent tissues
Include examples such as parenchyma, collenchyma, sclerenchyma
Label their functions
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of chart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Xylem and Phloem Function Activity
steps: Write the functions of xylem and phloem
Draw a simple diagram of vascular bundle
Explain how water and food move in plants
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates functions of xylem and phloem

activities_projects.title: Animal Tissue Classification Table
steps: List epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues
Write their functions
Give one example for each

activities_projects.title: Muscle Tissue Comparison
steps: Draw diagrams of striated, smooth and cardiac muscles
Compare their structure
Write location and function of each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies structural differences among muscle tissues

activities_projects.title: Neuron Diagram Activity
steps: Draw a large diagram of a neuron
Label parts like dendrite, axon, cell body
Write functions of each part
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands structure and function of neuron

activities_projects.title: Connective Tissue Examples
steps: List examples of connective tissues such as blood, bone and cartilage
Write their functions
Explain how they support the body
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies types and roles of connective tissues

activities_projects.title: Plant Tissue Model
steps: Prepare a simple 3D model showing plant tissues
Label epidermis, xylem and phloem
Explain the function of each

activities_projects.title: Tissue Function Matching Activity
steps: Write names of tissues on flash cards
Write their functions on separate cards
Match the correct tissue with its function
